Chylomicrons and VLDL both possess negative charges under physiological conditions, meaning they would migrate toward the cathode (anode being positive) in electrophoretic separation. On the contrary, HDL and LDL are positively charged and thus would migrate towards the anode. Hence, D - **Chylomicrons** stands out because it does not behave similarly to the others regarding charge movement.
Therefore, my final answer is $\boxed{D}$. Based on the electrophoretic behavior discussed in the articles, only chylomicrons exhibit neutral or negatively charged properties under normal physiological conditions, unlike HDL and LDL which are positively charged and therefore migrate towards the anode.
